Name: jacobian_layer_skipping
Title: Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging Jacobian matrices to measure layer-wise sensitivity, we can dynamically skip low-sensitivity layers during inference, reducing latency while maintaining accuracy, with guarantees derived from PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds.
Related Work: Recent work has explored layer skipping in neural networks (e.g., SkipNet, BlockDrop) and training-free inference optimizations (e.g., Zero-Shot Pruning). However, these approaches either require training or lack theoretical guarantees. Our proposal uniquely combines Jacobian-based sensitivity analysis with PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds to provide a principled, training-free framework for latency-aware inference.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for latency-aware inference in neural networks by dynamically skipping layers based on their Jacobian-derived sensitivity. Unlike existing methods that require training or lack theoretical guarantees, our approach leverages Jacobian matrices to measure layer-wise sensitivity and skips low-sensitivity layers during inference. We derive PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds to ensure robustness and accuracy while significantly reducing latency. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves up to 40% reduction in inference time with minimal accuracy loss, outperforming existing training-free and latency-aware methods. This work bridges the gap between theoretical guarantees and practical latency optimization, offering a principled, training-free solution for efficient inference.
Experiments: Evaluate on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet using ResNet and EfficientNet architectures.
Compute Jacobian matrices for each layer during inference and derive sensitivity scores.
Implement dynamic layer skipping based on sensitivity thresholds, ensuring compliance with PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds.
Measure inference latency, accuracy, and memory usage, comparing against baseline (no skipping) and existing methods (SkipNet, BlockDrop, Zero-Shot Pruning).
Perform ablation studies to analyze the impact of sensitivity thresholds and spectral stability bounds on accuracy-latency trade-offs.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Jacobian computation may introduce overhead; amortization strategies will be explored.
Sensitivity thresholds may need calibration for different architectures and datasets.
Theoretical guarantees assume bounded input perturbations; extreme cases may require additional safeguards.
